title: Daily Market Digest: May 13, 2026
subtitle: Market closes mixed amid inflation concerns and tech rebound

items.headline: Wall Street finishes mixed as inflation heats up, tech attempts rebound
items.summary: U.S. stock markets closed mixed on Wednesday, with major indices experiencing volatility. Stronger-than-expected inflation data for April weighed on investor sentiment, yet a robust rebound in technology stocks helped offset broader losses. Geopolitical concerns in the Middle East and upcoming U.S.-China talks further contributed to a cautious, yet active, trading session.

items.headline: UnitedHealth Group Inc.
items.summary: A major gainer for the Dow Jones Industrial Average.

items.headline: Nvidia
items.summary: Surged on reports of CEO Jensen Huang joining President Trump's delegation to China, potentially easing AI chip trade relations, and a reiterated outperform rating from analysts.

items.headline: Wix.com Ltd.
items.summary: Listed among the top daily losers, also reported earnings today.

items.headline: Inflation heats up, impacting Fed rate cut expectations
items.summary: U.S. headline CPI rose to 3.8% year-over-year in April 2026, surpassing forecasts and marking the fastest pace since 2023, primarily driven by an oil shock. Wholesale inflation (PPI) also jumped 6% annually, its biggest rise since 2022, signaling persistent input cost pressures. This hotter-than-expected data renews concerns that higher energy costs will pressure corporate earnings and may keep Federal Reserve rate cuts further away.

items.headline: Geopolitical tensions and U.S.-China summit in focus
items.summary: Uncertainty surrounding the Middle East conflict, particularly involving Iran, continues to affect global energy markets and investor sentiment. Simultaneously, President Donald Trump's high-profile visit to China to meet with President Xi Jinping is a key focus, with discussions expected to cover trade, artificial intelligence, and wider geopolitical tensions. The presence of Nvidia's CEO in the delegation hints at potential easing in US-China AI and technology trade relations.

items.headline: Semiconductors, Energy, and Industrials lead; Consumer sectors lag
items.summary: In sector performance, Semiconductors, Energy, Defense, and Industrials are notably leading the market, driven by robust AI infrastructure spending and a multi-year defense budget cycle. Conversely, Communication Services and Consumer Staples showed declines, with Commercial Real Estate, Discretionary, and Utilities also lagging due to rate sensitivity.

items.headline: Tower Semiconductor reports strong Q1; Several major companies report earnings
items.summary: Tower Semiconductor ($TSEM) reported a strong start to 2026, with Q1 revenue up 15% year-over-year to $414 million and net profit reaching $65 million. The company projects record-high Q2 revenue of $455 million. Similarweb ($SMWB) also matched its Q1 earnings estimates while exceeding revenue forecasts. Major companies such as Cisco Systems ($CSCO), Inovio Pharmaceuticals ($INO), Microvision ($MVIS), Toyota Motor ($TM), Wix ($WIX), Alibaba ($BABA), Eos Energy Enterprises ($EOSE), and Paysafe ($PSFE) were scheduled to report earnings today.
